    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp", "symbol": "buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h", "symbol": "OptimizationLevel"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h", "symbol": "RegisterPass"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h", "symbol": "INITIALIZE_PASS"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h", "symbol": "PassRegistry::registerPass"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h", "symbol": "PassInfo"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h", "symbol": "PassBuilder"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp", "symbol": "buildModuleOptimizationPipeline"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h", "symbol": "PassManager::addPass"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h", "symbol": "PassManager::run"},
    {"repo": "llvm/llvm-project", "path": "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h", "symbol": "PassConcept::run"}
  ],
  "text": "LLVM pass registration and execution follows a multi-layer architecture spanning from registration through execution order in the O2/O3 optimization pipeline.\n\n## Registration Phase\nPasses are registered via the PassSupport.h macros. The RegisterPass<> template (llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h:89) is the primary mechanism for registering new passes. This template creates a PassInfo object describing the pass and registers it with the global PassRegistry via PassRegistry::registerPass() (llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h:70). The INITIALIZE_PASS macro (llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h:56-58) provides an alternative mechanism for passes with dependencies, using once_flag synchronization to initialize passes exactly once.\n\nPassRegistry (llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h) maintains two maps: PassInfoMap (indexed by pass type ID) and PassInfoStringMap (indexed by pass argument names). This allows lookup by either the pass pointer address or the string name used on the command line.\n\n## PassManager Template and Structure\nThe core PassManager template is defined in llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:159-226. It is a template class parameterized by IRUnitT (Module, Function, etc.) and an AnalysisManagerT. The PassManager stores passes as a vector of PassConceptT pointers (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:225), implementing concept-based polymorphism to allow heterogeneous pass types.\n\nSpecialized typedefs are provided:\n- ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module> (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:238)\n- FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function> (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:247)\n\nThe addPass() method (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:193-214) accepts any pass type and wraps it in a PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...> object before storing it. This allows passes of different types to be added to the same manager.\n\n## Pass Execution Order\nPass execution happens in PassManager::run() (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h:28-98). The method iterates through the stored passes sequentially (line 67: for (auto &Pass : Passes)). For each pass:\n\n1. PassInstrumentation::runBeforePass() callbacks are invoked (line 73)\n2. The pass's run() method is called with the IR unit and AnalysisManager (line 76)\n3. The AnalysisManager invalidates analyses based on the pass's PreservedAnalyses result (line 80)\n4. PassInstrumentation::runAfterPass() callbacks are invoked (line 84)\n5. Preserved analyses are intersected across all passes (line 88)\n\nThis sequential execution ensures dependency ordering and analysis invalidation consistency.\n\n## O2/O3 Optimization Pipeline Construction\nThe PassBuilder class (llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h and llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp) constructs the optimization pipeline. The entry point for standard optimizations is buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase LTOPhase) defined in llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp:1478.\n\nThis function:\n1. Creates a ModulePassManager instance\n2. Adds module-level passes in sequence using MPM.addPass()\n3. Creates nested FunctionPassManager instances for function-level optimizations\n4. Creates LoopPassManager instances for loop optimizations\n5. Returns the constructed pipeline\n\nKey passes added in O3 that differ from O2 (llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp):\n- ControlHeightReductionPass (line 1565-1566, O3 only)\n- ArgumentPromotion (checked at line 1626: if (Level == OptimizationLevel::O3))\n- Various loop unrolling and vectorization passes with different thresholds\n\nThe buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline() function (llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp:592) handles function-level optimizations like SROA, Early CSE, Jump Threading, Reassociation, and loop passes. This pipeline is invoked within the module pipeline to process each function.\n\n## Pass Registration Database\nPassRegistry.def (llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def) defines all registered passes using MODULE_PASS, CGSCC_PASS, and FUNCTION_PASS macros. Each entry specifies the pass creation expression, enabling dynamic pass creation by the PassBuilder.\n\n## Analysis Management and Dependencies\nAnalysisManager (llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h:249+) caches analysis results and manages pass dependencies. It uses lazy evaluation: analyses are only computed when requested by passes that call getResult<AnalysisType>(). The PassInstrumentationAnalysis tracks pass execution for profiling and debugging.\n\nCGSCCPassManager (llvm/include/llvm/Analysis/CGSCCPassManager.h) provides inter-procedural optimization by iterating over strongly connected components of the call graph. LoopPassManager (llvm/include/llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h) handles loop-specific optimizations with special iteration and invalidation semantics for loop nests."
}
EOF
cat /workspace/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /workspace/answer.json > /dev/null && echo "Valid JSON format" && wc -l /workspace/answer.json

conversation_events.text:
# LLVM Pass Manager Registration and Execution Chain

## Your Task

Trace how a new LLVM optimization pass is registered and executed in llvm/llvm-project. Find C++ source files that define: the PassManager template, the PassRegistry, the pass add() registration, and the pass execution order in the standard optimization pipeline (O2/O3).

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossrepo domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d, sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d` (llvm/llvm-project)
- `sg-evals/gcc--96dfb333` (gcc-mirror/gcc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you trace the LLVM Pass Manager registration and execution chain. Let me start by exploring the repository structure to understand how passes are organized.
Now let me look at the execution order and how passes are added to the pipeline more directly:
Perfect! I've completed the analysis of LLVM pass registration and execution. Let me verify the answer file:

Excellent! I've successfully traced the LLVM pass registration and execution chain and created the answer at `/workspace/answer.json`.

## Summary of Findings

I've identified the complete execution chain for LLVM optimization passes:

### **Registration Phase (PassSupport.h)**
- `RegisterPass<>` template registers passes with `PassRegistry`
- `INITIALIZE_PASS` macros create `PassInfo` objects
- `PassRegistry::registerPass()` stores passes by type ID and argument name

### **PassManager Template (IR/PassManager.h)**
- Template class parameterized by IR unit type (Module, Function)
- Uses concept-based polymorphism with `PassConceptT`
- `addPass()` method wraps heterogeneous pass types in `PassModel`
- Provides `ModulePassManager` and `FunctionPassManager` typedefs

### **Pass Execution Order (IR/PassManagerImpl.h)**
- `PassManager::run()` iterates passes sequentially
- For each pass: invoke BeforePass callbacks → run pass → invalidate analyses → invoke AfterPass callbacks
- Intersects preserved analyses to maintain consistency

### **O2/O3 Pipeline Construction (Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp)**
- `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` is the main entry point (line 1478)
- `buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline()` handles function-level passes (line 592)
- O3-specific passes: `ControlHeightReductionPass`, `ArgumentPromotion`, aggressive unrolling
- Nested pipeline structure: Module → CGSCC → Function → Loop

### **Key Infrastructure Files**
- **17 files** identified across the codebase
- **19 symbols** documenting key classes and methods
- **9-step execution chain** from registration through execution

The answer file includes all relevant file paths, symbols, and a comprehensive narrative explaining the complete architecture.

  "text": "LLVM pass registration and execution follows a multi-layer architecture spanning from registration through execution order in the O2/O3 optimization pipeline.\n\n## Registration Phase\nPasses are registered via the PassSupport.h macros. The RegisterPass<> template (llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h:89) is the primary mechanism for registering new passes. This template creates a PassInfo object describing the pass and registers it with the global PassRegistry via PassRegistry::registerPass() (llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h:70).
